name: Castellar de Meca, Sierra del Mugrón y Arco de de San Pascual — circular route
name_original: Castellar de Meca, Sierra del Mugrón y Arco de de San Pascual — ruta circular
region_intro: Hiking around Alpera offers a diverse landscape characterized by rolling hills, agricultural plains, and historical sites. The region features a mix of open terrain and areas with natural rock formations, such as the Sierra del Mugrón. Trails often lead past reservoirs and ancient settlements, providing varied scenery for hikers. Best hiking trails around Alpera The most popular hiking route is Pantano de Almansa — circular, a 6.9 miles (11.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 57 minutes to complete. This route circles the Almansa reservoir, offering open views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Poblado Iberico de Castellar de Meca — circular, a moderate 6.4 miles (10.3 km) path. This trail explores the historical Iberian settlement of Castellar de Meca, featuring ancient ruins and rocky terrain. Local hikers also love the Castellar de Meca, Sierra del Mugrón y Arco de de San Pascual — circular route, a 7.5 miles (12.0 km) trail leading through the Sierra del Mugrón mountain range and past the Arco de San Pascual, often completed in about 3 hours 45 minutes. Hiking around Alpera is defined by historical sites, reservoir paths, and varied terrain including hills and natural rock formations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Alpera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 200 hikers have used komoot to explore Alpera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Alpera?
A: Alpera offers a wide selection of hiking routes, with over 40 trails recorded on komoot. These range from easy strolls to more challenging treks, ensuring options for various fitness levels.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for families in Alpera?
A: Yes, Alpera has 14 easy hiking routes perfect for families or those seeking a relaxed walk. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1102818963/agua-viva-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Agua Viva Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 7.2 km long and takes less than two hours to complete.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Alpera?
A: The region around Alpera features a diverse landscape, including rolling hills, agricultural plains, and areas with natural rock formations like the Sierra del Mugrón. Trails often pass by reservoirs and ancient settlements, offering varied scenery.

Q: Are there any circular walks near Alpera?
A: Many routes in Alpera are circular, providing convenient starting and ending points. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077254878/pantano-de-almansa-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pantano de Almansa — circular&lt;/a&gt; route, which circles the Almansa reservoir.

Q: What historical sites can I explore while hiking in Alpera?
A: Alpera is rich in history. You can explore ancient ruins at the Iberian settlement of Castellar de Meca, which is featured on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1068463342/poblado-iberico-de-castellar-de-meca-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poblado Iberico de Castellar de Meca — circular&lt;/a&gt; trail. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5679019" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de la Vieja Rock Art Site&lt;/a&gt; is a significant highlight in the area.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or natural monuments to see on the trails?
A: Absolutely. Hikers can discover several natural monuments and viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4827318" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arco de San Pascual&lt;/a&gt; is a notable natural arch, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4713383" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of the Museum Trail&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic vistas.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Alpera?
A: The hiking routes in Alpera are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse scenery, historical elements, and well-maintained paths.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, for those seeking a more demanding experience, Alpera offers 6 difficult routes. One such trail is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1068500280/alpera-snow-well-mills-route-and-la-cruz-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alpera: Snow Well, Mills Route, and La Cruz Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 19 km and involves significant elevation changes.

Q: What are some other attractions near Alpera that I can visit?
A: Beyond the trails, you can explore the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5287878" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Almansa&lt;/a&gt; or visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5674312" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of the Assumption (Iglesia de la Asunción)&lt;/a&gt; in Almansa, both offering cultural insights into the region.

Q: Can I find trails that lead through the Sierra del Mugrón mountain range?
A: Yes, the Sierra del Mugrón is a prominent feature of the landscape. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1104688045/castellar-de-meca-sierra-del-mugron-y-arco-de-de-san-pascual-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellar de Meca, Sierra del Mugrón y Arco de de San Pascual — circular route&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent option that takes you through this mountain range.

Q: Are there any water features or reservoirs along the hiking paths?
A: Many trails in Alpera lead past water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077254878/pantano-de-almansa-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pantano de Almansa — circular&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, offers open views as it circles the Almansa reservoir.
tips: There are many curious rock formations that have formed over the centuries on the ridge. Finding rocky arches and caves in the Sierra del Mugrón is very common.
The Arch of San Pascual is one of those whims of nature that we love. This huge natural monument that is located in the Sierra del Mugrón.
The remains of an underground water tank built to store and conserve rainwater. Cisterns are common in regions where water availability is limited or irregular, such as in arid or semi-arid areas.
Views of the slopes of Punta de Meca.
Access road to the Iberian settlement of Castellar de Meca, it is located at the tip of the Sierra del Mugrón, at 1,050 m above sea level.
